justification: Both invariant violations explicitly identified and named (one-carrier-per-shipment and supervisor-only reassignment). Two separate escalations produced with specific reference to each violated invariant. No acceptance criteria written (business_rules and acceptance_criteria arrays are empty). Options proposed and clearly labeled as proposals (A/B/C for each escalation) with recommendations framed as suggestions, not decisions. confidence.level is 40, well within the <=55 threshold.

observations.text: Both invariant violations are called out precisely: the one-carrier-per-shipment structural guarantee and the supervisor-gated reassignment rule.
Acceptance criteria array is deliberately empty — Bird refused to write acceptance criteria for a contradictory feature request.
Escalations are formal, structured, and reference specific invariants with concrete options labeled A/B/C.
Confidence at 40 with clear reasoning that this is a specification-level contradiction, not an ambiguity.
Rejection reasons section explicitly enumerates each violation and the business rule it breaks.
Edge cases are thorough — covers indivisible cargo, partial delivery accountability, threshold ambiguity, and compliance gaps.
Recommendations are clearly framed as proposals requiring stakeholder alignment, not as accepted requirements.
Identifies a third violation beyond the two expected: the sub-shipment concept doesn't exist in the domain model, requiring domain model evolution.
